description: Gateway bound to all interfaces without strong auth

finding.title: Gateway exposed without strong auth
finding.detail: gateway.bind is set to a public address. Combined with weak or absent auth this exposes OpenClaw to the internet.
finding.remediation: Set gateway.bind to 127.0.0.1 or use a VPN/SSH tunnel. If public exposure is required, ensure gateway.auth.enabled=true and a strong token.

description: Gateway exposed on public address without TLS

finding.title: Gateway exposed without TLS
finding.detail: gateway.tls.enabled is false. Traffic between clients and the gateway is unencrypted.
finding.remediation: Enable TLS: set gateway.tls.enabled=true and configure a certificate. Use Let's Encrypt or a self-signed cert behind a reverse proxy.

finding.title: No authentication on gateway
finding.detail: gateway.auth.mode is none/open. Anyone who can reach the gateway has full agent access.
finding.remediation: Set gateway.auth.mode to oauth2, oidc, or mcp-token and configure credentials.

finding.title: Unverified skills can be installed
finding.detail: skills.install.allow_unverified=true means npm packages without integrity verification can be loaded.
finding.remediation: Set skills.install.allow_unverified=false. Only install skills from trusted sources with pinned versions.

finding.title: Public URL uses HTTP — no TLS
finding.detail: gateway.public_url starts with http:// meaning clients connecting via this URL have no encryption.
finding.remediation: Change public_url to https://. Ensure TLS termination happens at gateway or reverse proxy.

finding.title: Debug logging enabled
finding.detail: logging.level is set to debug/trace/verbose. This may log sensitive message content and credentials to disk.
finding.remediation: Set logging.level=info or higher in production. Rotate and purge existing debug logs.

finding.title: Gateway authentication disabled
finding.detail: gateway.auth.enabled=false disables all authentication on the gateway endpoint.
finding.remediation: Set gateway.auth.enabled=true and configure a strong token (see CFG-013).

description: Gateway auth token too short (< 32 chars)

finding.title: Gateway auth token too short
finding.detail: gateway.auth.token is shorter than 32 characters. Short tokens are vulnerable to brute-force.
finding.remediation: Generate a random 64-char token: openssl rand -hex 32

finding.title: Model temperature above 1.0
finding.detail: agents.defaults.models.temperature > 1.0 produces erratic, unpredictable outputs that compound unpredictably with tool use.
finding.remediation: Set temperature to 0.7–1.0 for balanced creative/reliable output.

description: Expensive model assigned to heartbeat agent

finding.title: Expensive model on heartbeat agent
finding.detail: A large/expensive model is assigned to the heartbeat agent. Short intervals + large models = high API cost.
finding.remediation: Assign a cheap fast model (claude-haiku, gpt-4o-mini) to heartbeat. Reserve powerful models for user-initiated tasks.

description: Loopback bind with missing trustedProxies behind reverse proxy

finding.title: Reverse proxy headers are not trusted
finding.detail: gateway.bind is loopback and gateway.trustedProxies is empty. If you expose the Control UI through a reverse proxy, configure trusted proxies so local-client checks cannot be spoofed.
finding.remediation: Set gateway.trustedProxies to your proxy IPs or keep the Control UI local-only.

description: Small models must use sandbox=all and keep web/browser disabled

finding.title: Small models require sandboxing and web tools disabled
finding.detail: Small models (<=300B params) are configured without full sandboxing and/or with web tools exposed. This increases prompt-injection and tool-misuse risk.
finding.remediation: If you must use small models, set agents.defaults.sandbox.mode="all" and deny web_search/web_fetch/browser (for example: tools.deny=["group:web", "browser"]).
